mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of July 2009 indicates recent easing from crisis peaks
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 9.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in July 2009 reflects post-crisis labor recovery
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth exceeding 2%, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 700291M and HOUST at 1460K units in June 2009 show early recovery
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 100 index points signal supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization, reducing corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.57 in July 2009 shows recovery from crisis lows
failure_condition: When INDPRO is below 100.0, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly measure labor market momentum. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month while UNRATE declines, equity markets price in sustained consumer income growth and positive earnings revisions.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 0.1% month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157700K and UNRATE at 3.7% in July 2009
regime_hint: labor market recovery with employment gains
failure_condition: When both conditions trigger, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or the interaction term has negative coefficient with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraints and potential rate hikes, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 309.685 in July 2009 reflects post-crisis disinflation
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as the primary demand signal. Quarters with real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized indicate economic stagnation that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22672.859B (2017 dollars) in Q1 2009 reflects recession trough
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is below 1.5% annualized, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) combined with industrial production (INDPRO) form a composite demand-supply signal. When HOUST and RSAFS accelerate while INDPRO remains above 101, the market prices in sustained demand without supply constraints, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 1.5% AND INDPRO > 101.0
evidence_refs: fact:All three series show recovery trajectory in mid-2009
regime_hint: balanced recovery with demand acceleration and adequate supply capacity
failure_condition: When all three trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 62% of instances, or the three-way interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).
